description:
Landing is failing because the worktree contains uncommitted changes, while the build/review flow is also asking for `context.md` in ways that are not meant to be committed. The sequencing around scratch handoff files vs. final land needs to be corrected.

Scope:
- identify where `context.md` or similar transient handoff artifacts are created/updated during delegate/review/land
- ensure transient files do not poison the final land step with uncommitted-change failures
- either relocate, clean, ignore, or explicitly manage those artifacts as part of the landing sequence
- add regression coverage for a successful land when transient context files are present in the expected flow

Notes:
- this overlaps with the other landing-flow tickets; prefer landing-lane work to happen in the same branch/worker sequence
- do not weaken genuine dirty-tree protection for real source changes

Definition of done:
- tests added/updated under `packages/pi-beadwork-extension/src/__tests__`
- `npm run lint -w @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension`
- `npm run test -w @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension`
- `npm run typecheck -w @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension`
title: Fix `land` dirty-worktree failures caused by generated `context.md` handoff files
